prophecies.a:
荡荡天门万古开，几人归去几人来。
山河虽好非完璧，不信黄金是祸胎。

白话文翻译：天门自万古以来敞开，多少人离去又多少人到来。山河虽然美好却已非完璧，不相信黄金竟是祸患的根源。
prophecies.a_en:
The gates of heaven have stood open since time immemorial; how many depart, how many arrive.
The mountains and rivers are fine, yet no longer whole; who would believe that gold is the seed of disaster.

Vernacular: The heavenly gates have been open since ancient times — how many leave and how many come? Though the land is beautiful, it is no longer intact; no one believed that gold would be the root of calamity.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「山河虽好非完璧」指北宋虽繁荣但已有隐患。「黄金是祸胎」暗指金国（「金」）入侵，靖康二年（1127年）金兵攻破汴京，俘虏徽钦二帝，北宋灭亡，史称靖康之变。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Mountains and rivers fine yet no longer whole' refers to the Northern Song's hidden vulnerabilities despite its prosperity. 'Gold is the seed of disaster' is a pun on the Jin (金, 'Gold') dynasty invasion. In 1127, Jin forces sacked the capital Kaifeng and captured Emperors Huizong and Qinzong, ending the Northern Song — the Jingkang Incident.

prophecies.a:
湖山一梦事全非，再见云龙向北飞。
三百年来终一日，长天碧水叹弥弥。

白话文翻译：西湖畔如同一场大梦万事全非，再次看见云龙向北方飞去。三百年的基业终归有尽头的一天，在漫漫长天碧水中只余叹息无边。
prophecies.a_en:
By the lake and hills, a dream — everything has changed; once more the cloud-dragon flies northward.
Three hundred years come down to a single day; beneath the vast sky and blue waters, endless sighs.

Vernacular: Life by the West Lake was like a grand dream — everything has changed. Once again the cloud-dragon soars northward. Three hundred years of dynastic legacy end in a single day; under the boundless sky and blue waters, only endless sighs remain.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「湖山一梦」指南宋偏安临安（杭州西湖），沉醉享乐如同一梦。「云龙向北飞」指宋朝气数北去。「三百年来终一日」指两宋合计约三百二十年国祚至此终结。1279年崖山之战，陆秀夫背幼帝投海，南宋灭亡。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Lake and hills, a dream' refers to the Southern Song's retreat to Lin'an (Hangzhou, by West Lake), living in complacent luxury. 'Cloud-dragon flies north' indicates the dynasty's fate departing. 'Three hundred years end in a single day' — the two Song dynasties lasted about 320 years total. In 1279, at the Battle of Yamen, Lu Xiufu carried the young emperor into the sea, ending the Southern Song.

prophecies.a:
天地相乘数一原，忽逢甲子又兴元。
年华二八乘除尽，一枕梅花梦未残。

白话文翻译：天地相乘之数归于一元，忽然逢到甲子年又兴起了元朝。年华以二八之数相乘除而尽，枕上梅花一梦尚未醒来。
prophecies.a_en:
Heaven and earth multiply — all numbers trace to one origin; suddenly a jiazi year arrives and Yuan arises.
The years of twice-eight are multiplied and divided to their end; a pillow of plum blossoms, the dream not yet faded.

Vernacular: The numbers of heaven and earth multiply back to a single origin; suddenly in a jiazi year, the Yuan dynasty rises. The years counted as twice-eight are multiplied and divided to their end; amid plum blossoms on the pillow, the dream has not yet faded.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「兴元」直指元朝建立。「甲子又兴元」暗指元世祖忽必烈建元。「年华二八乘除尽」指元朝国祚，二八为十六，也有说法指元朝传十五帝、约九十八年。1368年朱元璋建立明朝，元朝覆灭。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Yuan arises' directly names the Yuan dynasty. 'In a jiazi year, Yuan arises' alludes to Kublai Khan founding the Yuan. 'Twice-eight multiplied and divided' refers to the Yuan's lifespan — 16 by some reckoning, or 15 emperors over about 98 years. In 1368, Zhu Yuanzhang founded the Ming dynasty, ending Mongol rule.

prophecies.a:
毕竟英雄起布衣，朱门不是旧黄畿。
飞来燕子寻常事，开到李花春已非。

白话文翻译：毕竟真英雄从布衣百姓中崛起，朱姓的皇门已非旧日的黄土疆域。飞来的燕子不过是寻常之事，等到李花盛开之时春天已经不在了。
prophecies.a_en:
After all, the hero rises from common cloth; the vermilion gate is no longer the old yellow domain.
A swallow flying in is an ordinary affair; by the time the plum blossoms open, spring is already gone.

Vernacular: After all, the true hero rises from humble origins; the house of Zhu is no longer the old territory. A swallow flying in is just an ordinary affair; by the time the plum blossoms open, spring has already passed.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「英雄起布衣」指朱元璋出身贫寒建立明朝。「朱门」直指朱姓皇室。「飞来燕子」指燕王朱棣发动靖难之役夺取帝位。「开到李花春已非」指李自成起义攻破北京，明朝覆灭（1644年）。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Hero rises from common cloth' refers to Zhu Yuanzhang's humble origins in founding the Ming. 'Vermilion gate' directly names the Zhu imperial house. 'A swallow flying in' alludes to the Prince of Yan (Zhu Di) seizing the throne in the Jingnan Campaign. 'Plum blossoms open, spring is gone' — 'Li' (plum/李) refers to Li Zicheng's rebellion capturing Beijing in 1644, ending the Ming.

prophecies.a:
胡儿骑马走长安，开辟中原海境宽。
洪水乍平洪水起，清光宜向汉中看。

白话文翻译：胡人骑马占据了长安，开辟中原将版图扩展到海疆。洪水刚刚平息又有洪水再起，清朝的光芒应当向汉中地方去看。
prophecies.a_en:
The northern horsemen ride into Chang'an, opening the Central Plain and widening the sea frontier.
One flood barely subsides before another rises; the light of Qing should be sought toward Hanzhong.

Vernacular: The northern horsemen ride into Chang'an and open up the Central Plain, extending the territory to the seas. One flood barely subsides before another rises; the glory of the Qing should be looked for in the direction of Hanzhong.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「胡儿骑马走长安」指满族（胡人）入关建立清朝。「开辟中原海境宽」指清朝扩张版图。「洪水乍平洪水起」指洪秀全太平天国运动。「清光宜向汉中看」暗示清朝最终被汉人推翻，1912年辛亥革命建立中华民国。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Northern horsemen ride into Chang'an' refers to the Manchu conquest establishing the Qing dynasty. 'Opening the Central Plain, widening the sea frontier' describes Qing territorial expansion. 'One flood barely subsides before another rises' — 'flood' (洪) alludes to Hong Xiuquan's Taiping Rebellion. 'The light of Qing should be sought toward Hanzhong' hints that the Qing would ultimately be overthrown by the Han Chinese; the 1911 Revolution established the Republic of China in 1912.

prophecies.a:
汉天一白汉江秋，憔悴黄花总带愁。
吉曜半升箕斗隐，金乌起灭海山头。

白话文翻译：汉人的天空一片苍白，汉江之畔秋意萧瑟。憔悴的菊花（黄花）满含忧愁。吉祥的星曜才升到一半，箕斗星便隐没。金乌（太阳）在海山之巅起而复灭。
prophecies.a_en:
The Han sky turns pale white, autumn on the Han River; withered yellow flowers all carry sorrow.
The auspicious star rises halfway as the Winnowing Basket hides; the golden crow rises and sets over the sea-mountain summit.

Vernacular: The sky of the Han people turns pale, autumn descends on the Han River. Withered yellow chrysanthemums are filled with sorrow. The auspicious star has only half risen when the Winnowing Basket constellation hides; the golden crow (sun) rises and falls at the summit of the sea-mountains.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「汉天一白」指民国时期汉人当政。「憔悴黄花总带愁」指民国内忧外患，黄花岗起义等革命艰辛。「吉曜半升」暗指中华民国政权尚未稳固。「金乌起灭海山头」指日本（海东之国）的侵华与最终战败（1937-1945），「金乌」象征日本国旗中的太阳。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Han sky turns pale' refers to the Republican era under Han Chinese rule. 'Withered yellow flowers carry sorrow' alludes to the hardships of the Republic — revolutions like the Huanghuagang Uprising. 'Auspicious star rises halfway' suggests the Republic's government was never fully stable. 'Golden crow rises and sets over the sea-mountain' refers to Japan (the island nation to the east) — its invasion of China and ultimate defeat (1937-1945). The 'golden crow' symbolizes the sun on Japan's flag.

prophecies.a:
云雾苍茫各一天，可怜西北起烽烟。
东来暴客西来盗，还有胡儿在眼前。

白话文翻译：云雾苍茫之中各据一方天地，可叹西北方又燃起了烽火狼烟。从东边来了凶暴的侵客，从西边来了掠夺的强盗，更有胡人的威胁近在眼前。
prophecies.a_en:
Clouds and mist stretch vast — each side holds its own sky; how pitiful that war fires rise in the northwest.
Violent guests come from the east, thieves from the west; and still the northern horsemen stand before our eyes.

Vernacular: Amid vast clouds and mist, each side occupies its own domain; how pitiful that war fires ignite in the northwest. Violent invaders come from the east, plunderers from the west; and the threat of the northern horsemen remains right before our eyes.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「云雾苍茫各一天」被解读为国共分治、两岸隔海相望。「西北起烽烟」指朝鲜战争（1950年）或中印边境冲突。「东来暴客」指日本或美国势力，「西来盗」指苏联或西方列强，「胡儿在眼前」指北方边境的威胁。此首解读争议较大，不同注家有不同说法。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Each side holds its own sky' is interpreted as the KMT-CCP split — mainland and Taiwan separated across the strait. 'War fires in the northwest' may refer to the Korean War (1950) or Sino-Indian border conflicts. 'Violent guests from the east' may mean Japan or US forces; 'thieves from the west' may mean the Soviet Union or Western powers; 'northern horsemen' refers to threats on the northern border. Interpretations of this poem vary significantly among commentators.

prophecies.a:
如棋世事局初残，共济和衷却大难。
豹死犹留皮一袭，最佳秋色在长安。

白话文翻译：世事如棋局已进入残局阶段，同舟共济齐心协力方能渡过大难。豹虽死去却留下一袭虎皮，最美好的秋天景色就在长安（中国）。
prophecies.a_en:
The affairs of the world are like a chess game entering its endgame; only by working together in harmony can the great crisis be overcome.
The leopard dies but leaves behind its pelt; the finest autumn colors are in Chang'an.

Vernacular: World affairs are like a chess game reaching its final phase; only by joining forces in unity can the great calamity be overcome. The leopard dies yet leaves behind its skin; the most beautiful autumn scenery is to be found in Chang'an (China).
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「如棋世事局初残」被解读为当今世界格局进入变局阶段。「共济和衷却大难」暗示需要团结合作应对危机。「豹死犹留皮一袭」意为旧势力虽然衰落，其影响犹存。「最佳秋色在长安」被解读为中国（长安为古代中国象征）将迎来复兴。此首指向近现代，解读争议较大。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Chess game entering its endgame' is interpreted as the current world order entering a period of transformation. 'Working together to overcome crisis' suggests unity is needed to face challenges. 'Leopard dies but leaves its pelt' implies that old powers may decline but their influence persists. 'Finest autumn in Chang'an' is interpreted as China (Chang'an symbolizing China) heading toward revival. This poem points to the modern/contemporary era and interpretations vary widely.

prophecies.a:
火龙蛰起燕门秋，原璧应难赵氏收。
一院奇花春有主，连宵风雨不须愁。

白话文翻译：火龙从蛰伏中苏醒，燕门秋风萧瑟。原本的璧玉恐怕难以被赵氏收回。满院奇花到了春天自有其主人，即使连夜风雨也不必忧愁。
prophecies.a_en:
The fire dragon stirs from hibernation in the autumn of Yanmen; the original jade disc may be hard for the Zhao clan to reclaim.
A courtyard of rare flowers — spring has its master; even through nights of wind and rain, there is no need for sorrow.

Vernacular: The fire dragon awakens from its slumber in the autumn of Yanmen; the original jade disc will be hard for the Zhao clan to reclaim. A courtyard full of rare flowers will have its rightful master in spring; even through continuous nights of wind and rain, there is no need to worry.

prophecies.a:
数点梅花天地春，欲将剥复问前因。
寰中自有承平日，四海为家孰主宾。

白话文翻译：几朵梅花点缀之间天地迎来了春天，若要探问剥极而复的前因后果。天下之中自然有太平安宁的日子到来，四海为家之时谁是主人谁是宾客已不重要。
prophecies.a_en:
A few plum blossoms herald spring between heaven and earth; let us ask the causes behind the cycles of decline and renewal.
Within the world, days of peace will surely come; when all seas are home, who is host and who is guest?

Vernacular: A few plum blossoms usher in spring across heaven and earth; if one asks about the causes of the cycles of decline and renewal. Within the world, days of peace and prosperity will naturally arrive; when the whole world is home, the distinction between host and guest no longer matters.
